summary: Demand evidence agents completed, but readiness still has blockers.

demand_status.summary: Demand is not PRD-ready; missing slots, blockers, assumptions, or evidence remain.

triage.explanation: Fast route by default; no hard factual risk trigger was found.

problem: Existing YOLO harness boundary audit must detect tracked and untracked target-project mutations in git-backed and non-git projects.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Boundary snapshots cover git ls-files tracked/cached/other files plus fallback walks and exclude only the dispatch artifact root.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Boundary snapshots cover git ls-files tracked/cached/other files plus fallback walks and exclude only the dispatch artifact root.
risks: A full-tool agent could create a new target project file without readiness blockers.

verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.

agent_results.claim: The demand evidence dispatch boundary snapshot system correctly covers tracked, cached, and other git files while excluding only the dispatch artifact root.

evidence.summary: gitFiles() runs 'git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z' which covers tracked (-c cached) and untracked (-o others) files
evidence.why: This is the primary boundary coverage mechanism for git-backed projects

evidence.summary: walkFiles() provides recursive directory fallback with exclusions for .git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache
evidence.why: This ensures non-git projects also have boundary coverage

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() uses gitFiles() || walkFiles() and filters out allowedRoots (artifact directories)
evidence.why: This is the core boundary detection logic that combines coverage with artifact exclusion

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots() compares before/after and reports added, modified, or deleted files
evidence.why: This provides detection of mutations that occurred during agent execution

evidence.summary: Test 'git-backed boundary detects untracked project mutations' creates untracked file and verifies detection
evidence.why: This is evidence that git untracked file detection is tested and working

evidence.summary: Test 'non-git boundary fallback detects modified project files and ignores artifacts' covers walkFiles() behavior
evidence.why: This is evidence that the non-git fallback path is tested and correctly excludes artifacts

evidence.summary: Test 'boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass' enforces boundary violations as blockers
evidence.why: This is evidence that boundary violations correctly block PRD readiness

evidence.summary: Current working tree is dirty with unstaged modifications (visible via 'git status --short' showing ' M' prefixes)
evidence.why: This confirms the status_quo claim that evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree

evidence.summary: Multiple evidence dispatch directories exist (dispatch-20260531*, dispatch-20260601*)
evidence.why: This is evidence that evidence dispatch has been run multiple times in this project

evidence.summary: excludedDir() blocks .git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache from walkFiles() traversal
evidence.why: This shows the exclusion list is hardcoded and does not exclude all possible artifact directories

assumptions: The git ls-files -co command covers all three categories mentioned (tracked, cached, other) but 'cached' in the claim may refer to git's staged state rather than the -c flag
Full-tool agent stress tests have not been run against the current implementation (only simulated via test mocks)
The hardcoded exclusion list in excludedDir() may not cover all project-specific artifact directories that should be excluded
risks: If git ls-files behavior differs across git versions, boundary coverage may be inconsistent
A sophisticated full-tool agent could create and delete files within a single execution session without detection if timing allows deletion before boundary snapshot
Projects with custom artifact directories not in the hardcoded exclusion list may have false positive boundary violations
missing: Stress test evidence with real Claude -p agents in full-tool mode
Test coverage for dirty working tree scenarios with staged but unstaged changes
Test coverage for boundary detection when agent creates and deletes files within the same execution session

result.notes: Boundary snapshot implementation correctly uses git ls-files -co for tracked/untracked coverage with walkFiles() fallback. Artifact exclusion works via allowedRoots filter. Test coverage exists for git-backed and non-git paths. Missing: real agent stress tests and edge cases like create-then-delete within session.

agent_results.claim: The explorer's evidence is factually grounded and properly distinguishes between verified facts and assumptions. The boundary snapshot system implementation exists with test coverage, but real-agent stress testing remains missing.

evidence.summary: Verified: gitFiles() implementation matches explorer claim with 'git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z'
evidence.why: Confirms the primary boundary coverage mechanism exists as described

evidence.summary: Verified: Test 'boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass' enforces boundary violations
evidence.why: Confirms that boundary violations correctly block PRD readiness as explorer claimed

evidence.summary: Verified: Current dispatch artifact root exists and is excluded from boundary snapshots
evidence.why: Confirms the exclusion mechanism for dispatch artifacts is working

evidence.summary: Verified: buildBoundarySnapshot() filters files against allowedRoots set containing artifact root
evidence.why: Confirms the artifact exclusion logic exists in code

assumptions: The explorer correctly identified that 'cached' in the PRD claim may not match the -c flag behavior (git ls-files -c shows cached/tracked files, not staged changes)
Real full-tool agent stress tests remain absent as explorer noted
Dirty working tree status_quo is verified from git status but staged-vs-unstaged distinction is not tested
risks: The term 'cached' in the acceptance criteria may not map precisely to git ls-files -c behavior, creating semantic ambiguity
Without real-agent stress tests, sophisticated file create-then-delete patterns remain unverified
Projects using custom artifact directories not in hardcoded exclusion list may encounter false positives
missing: Real Claude -p agent stress test results with full tools enabled
Test coverage for boundary detection with staged changes in dirty working tree
Test coverage for agent patterns that create and delete files within single session
Clarification of whether 'cached' in acceptance criteria means git staged state or git tracked state

result.notes: Explorer evidence is factually grounded with proper source paths. No assumption was promoted to fact. Missing data (real-agent stress tests, staged-change edge cases) is correctly identified. The semantic ambiguity of 'cached' in the acceptance criteria warrants clarification but does not block proceeding to PRD generation.
